_note: SPOUSE-PARENTS batch b_1000BC_0_1: documented parents (and full siblings where sourced) for married-in spouses that had NO parents. Verified against the existing tree so no node is double-parented; every child listed here was confirmed a root before writing. Existing-id references: JORAM (King Joram of Judah, father of Jehosheba), PER_ARTAXERXES1 (father of Parysatis, restated with new spouse Andia to append her), MAC_PHILIP_ANTIGONE + BERENICE1 (parents of Magas, restated to append him beside their existing daughter Antigone). One consolidated union: the regent Antipater (SPB_ANTIPATER) fathers three batch spouses — Cassander, Phila, Eurydice. New nodes use the SPB_ prefix. Placeholder unknown mothers/fathers are '—'. Skipped (no documented parents): QUEEN_SHEBA, SOLOMON_FOREIGN_WIVES, JEHOIADA, HEPHZIBAH, GRK_EXAMYES, GRK_CLEOBULINE, GRK_MNESARCHUS, GRK_PYTHAIS, ATT_ATTALUS_TIUM, MAC_PHILINNA, MAC_NICESIPOLIS, ATT_BOA, GRK_PYTHIAS_ELDER (adopted; birth parents unknown), PER_STATEIRA1 (parentage disputed), MAC_PHILIP_ANTIGONE (own parents unknown), MAC_CHRYSEIS (identity/parentage disputed), MAC_AUDATA (father unnamed). Sources: 2 Kings/2 Chron for biblical fathers; Herodotus, Ctesias (via Photius), Plutarch, Diogenes Laertius, Appian, Justin for the classical figures. Web/text-verified 2026-07-16.

families.note: 2 Chron 22:11 / 2 Kings 11:2 — Jehosheba 'daughter of the king' Joram; mother unrecorded (tradition holds she was not Athaliah's daughter, since she saved Joash from Athaliah's purge)

a.label:
Zadok
c.820 BC · of Judah; father of Jerusha the queen of Uzziah · 2 Kings 15:33, 2 Chron 27:1

families.note: 2 Kings 15:33 — Jerusha daughter of Zadok

a.label:
Zechariah
c.785 BC · of Judah; father of Abijah the mother of King Hezekiah · 2 Chron 29:1

families.note: 2 Chron 29:1 / 2 Kings 18:2 — Abi(jah) daughter of Zechariah

a.label:
Haruz of Jotbah
c.725 BC · of Jotbah in Judah; father of Meshullemeth the queen of Manasseh · 2 Kings 21:19

families.note: 2 Kings 21:19 — Meshullemeth daughter of Haruz of Jotbah

a.label:
Adaiah of Bozkath
c.675 BC · of Bozkath in Judah; father of Jedidah the mother of King Josiah · 2 Kings 22:1

families.note: 2 Kings 22:1 — Jedidah daughter of Adaiah of Bozkath

a.label:
Jeremiah of Libnah
c.655 BC · of Libnah in Judah (not the prophet); father of Hamutal, wife of Josiah & mother of two kings · 2 Kings 23:31, 24:18

families.note: 2 Kings 23:31 & 24:18 — Hamutal daughter of Jeremiah of Libnah

a.label:
Pedaiah of Rumah
c.655 BC · of Rumah; father of Zebidah, a wife of Josiah & mother of King Jehoiakim · 2 Kings 23:36

families.note: 2 Kings 23:36 — Zebidah daughter of Pedaiah of Rumah

a.label:
Cimon Coalemos
c.575 BC · Athenian of the Philaid house, thrice Olympic chariot-victor; father of Miltiades the Younger · Herodotus 6.103

children.label:
Stesagoras
c.555 BC · Philaid; elder brother of Miltiades the Younger, ruled the Thracian Chersonese before him · Herodotus 6.38-39

families.note: Herodotus 6.34-39, 103 — Miltiades & Stesagoras sons of Cimon Coalemos

a.label:
Olorus
c.570 BC · Thracian king; father of Hegesipyle, whose line ran to the historian Thucydides · Herodotus 6.39, Thucydides 4.104

families.note: Herodotus 6.39 — Hegesipyle daughter of the Thracian king Olorus

a.label:
Ariphron
c.555 BC · Athenian of the deme Cholargos; father of the statesman Xanthippus · Herodotus 6.131, 7.33

families.note: Herodotus 6.131 — Xanthippus son of Ariphron

a.label:
Otanes
c.550 BC · Persian noble; father of Queen Amestris, wife of Xerxes I · Herodotus 7.61

families.note: Herodotus 7.61 — Amestris daughter of Otanes

a.label:
Alcibiades the elder
c.510 BC · Athenian of the Salaminioi; father of Cleinias & grandfather of the famous Alcibiades · Isocrates 16, Andocides 4

families.note: Cleinias son of Alcibiades (the elder), of the recurring Athenian name-line

b.label:
Andia of Babylon
c.490 BC · a Babylonian consort of Artaxerxes I; mother of Parysatis · Ctesias (Persica, via Photius)

families.note: Ctesias — Parysatis daughter of Artaxerxes I by the Babylonian Andia; existing Artaxerxes restated to append her

a.label:
Axiochus
c.500 BC · of Miletus; father of Aspasia, the partner of Pericles · Plutarch, Pericles 24

families.note: Plutarch, Pericles 24 — Aspasia daughter of Axiochus of Miletus

a.label:
Aristocles
c.490 BC · Athenian; father of Ariston, the father of Plato; the family claimed descent from King Codrus · legendary pedigree · Diogenes Laertius 3.1

families.note: Diogenes Laertius 3.1 — Ariston son of Aristocles; Codrus descent legendary (flagged)

a.label:
Glaucon
c.485 BC · Athenian of the house of Dropides & Solon; father of Perictione (mother of Plato) & of Charmides · Diogenes Laertius 3.1, Plato Charmides

children.label:
Charmides
c.450 BC · Athenian, brother of Perictione & uncle of Plato; one of the Thirty Tyrants, fell at Piraeus 403 BC · Plato Charmides, Xenophon Hellenica 2.4

families.note: Diogenes Laertius 3.1 — Perictione & Charmides children of Glaucon

a.label:
Nicomachus
c.415 BC · of Stagira; physician to King Amyntas III of Macedon & father of Aristotle · Diogenes Laertius 5.1

b.label:
Phaestis
c.410 BC · of Chalcis; wife of Nicomachus & mother of Aristotle · Diogenes Laertius 5.1

children.label:
Arimneste
c.382 BC · sister of Aristotle · Diogenes Laertius 5.1, Sextus Empiricus

Arimnestus
c.380 BC · brother of Aristotle · Diogenes Laertius 5.1

families.note: Diogenes Laertius 5.1 — Aristotle son of Nicomachus & Phaestis; siblings Arimneste & Arimnestus

a.label:
Corrhagus
c.390 BC · Macedonian noble; father of Stratonice, wife of Antigonus I Monophthalmus · attested via her patronymic

families.note: Stratonice daughter of Corrhagus

a.label:
Menon of Pharsalus
c.385 BC · Thessalian commander of Pharsalus; father of Phthia, wife of Aeacides of Epirus · Plutarch, Pyrrhus 1

families.note: Plutarch, Pyrrhus 1 — Phthia daughter of Menon of Pharsalus

a.label:
Antipater
c.397 BC · Macedonian general & regent of Macedon under Philip II & Alexander; left in charge of Europe during the Persian campaign · father of Cassander, Phila, Eurydice & others · historical (Diodorus, Plutarch)

families.note: Diodorus/Plutarch — Cassander, Phila & Eurydice all documented children of the regent Antipater

a.label:
Philip
c.410 BC · Macedonian noble of Elimeia; father of Antigonus I Monophthalmus · historical

families.note: Antigonus I son of Philip (of Elimeia)

a.label:
Achaeus
c.330 BC · Anatolian dynast of the Seleucid house; father of Antiochis (mother of Attalus I) & of Laodice · per tradition · Strabo 13.4

families.note: Antiochis, mother of Attalus I, held to be daughter of Achaeus the elder — Seleucid descent (flagged per tradition)

a.label:
Agathocles
c.390 BC · a Thessalian settled at Pella in Macedon; father of Lysimachus the Diadochus · historical

families.note: Lysimachus son of Agathocles of Pella

a.label:
Antiochus
c.390 BC · a general of Philip II of Macedon; father of Seleucus I Nicator · Appian, Syriaca 57; Justin 15.4

b.label:
Laodice
c.385 BC · wife of the general Antiochus & mother of Seleucus I Nicator · Appian, Syriaca 57

families.note: Appian/Justin — Seleucus I son of Antiochus & Laodice

a.label:
Polycleitus of Larissa
c.295 BC · Thessalian noble of Larissa; father of Olympias, wife of Demetrius the Fair · historical

families.note: Olympias daughter of Polycleitus of Larissa
Magas of Cyrene son of Berenice I by her first husband Philip, hence stepson of Ptolemy I; existing pair restated to append him beside their daughter Antigone
